product_identity.name: Crewly
product_identity.type: social trip coordination and pooled funding platform
product_identity.core_problem: Group trips are difficult to coordinate because planning, pooled funds, receipts, private side expenses, and updates are scattered across disconnected tools.
product_identity.core_promise: One trip hub for public support, private planning, receipt-backed expense transparency, and subgroup activity.

strategic_intent.primary_outcome: Enable a trip organizer to coordinate travel, collect support, and manage private and public collaboration without scope confusion.

recommendations: Keep trip and private event ledgers clearly separated in product language and navigation.
Make original receipt values visible whenever translated text or converted display values are shown.
Default all media and detailed trip information to private unless explicitly promoted by the organizer.
optimization_recommendations: Use display-only currency conversion in MVP.
Keep event payment tracking status-based rather than transactional.
Preserve organizer-controlled public promotion of photos and updates.

answered_questions: Primary user is the Trip Organizer.
Trip fund uses a GoFundMe-style payment feature with organizer-controlled payout.
Receipt scanning uses both manual correction and automatic extraction.
Public can view selected trips and contribute, but only approved members can see full trip details.
Members besides the organizer can upload receipts and log expenses in MVP.
Trip photos are member-only by default, with organizer-controlled optional public highlights.
Approved trip members can freely create private events.
Private event payment handling is tracking-only in MVP.
Private event expenses stay separate from the main trip ledger by default.
Receipt translation and currency conversion are included, with conversion used for display only and a trip-level default.
assumptions_made: Trip contributions are treated as trip support rather than lending.
Supporters do not need access to full trip details to contribute.
Original receipt values remain the source of truth for financial records.
confidence_gaps: Payment compliance and refund policy validation still belongs in Stage 2.

normalized_product_model.receipt_localization_model: translated text and display-only FX layered over original source-of-truth data

product_topology.public_surface: selected trips with contributions and updates

remaining_gaps: Validate payment processor and compliance fit for organizer-controlled pooled contributions.
Validate contributor and member trust in the public/private financial model.
Validate comprehension of localized receipt data and ledger separation.

completion_status.reason: Stage 1 outputs are complete, coherent, and ready for validation-focused Stage 2 work.
next_actions: Validate organizer and supporter trust in pooled funding.
Validate user understanding of trip-versus-event financial separation.
Validate usability of localized receipt views.

completion_status.reason: All ten Stage 1 outputs exist, critical product questions are resolved, and remaining unknowns are validation targets rather than planning blockers.
next_actions: Run Stage 2 research and validation against the recorded assumptions and risk areas.
